title: tree-sitter-go-aco
description:
Restructure advanceVersion to match C's flat action loop semantics (parser.c:1620-1677).

INVESTIGATION COMPLETE - Root cause found:
1. extract.go:parseActionMacros groups actions by type (SHIFT before REDUCE) instead of preserving C source order. This inverts [REDUCE, SHIFT] to [SHIFT, REDUCE].
2. Go's extras-on-splits architecture processes ALL extras regardless of primary type, while C's flat loop terminates on SHIFT/ACCEPT/RECOVER.

FIX REQUIRES (both together, not separately):
1. Fix parseActionMacros in extract.go to preserve source position ordering (position-based sort)
2. Restructure advanceVersion to match C's flat loop:
   - Process actions sequentially (not primary + extras on splits)
   - REDUCE creates new versions via non-destructive pop (like C's ts_stack_pop_count)
   - SHIFT operates on original version and terminates the loop
   - After loop, ts_stack_renumber_version replaces original with last reduce's version
3. Regenerate ALL grammar parse tables after fixing the generator

IMPORTANT: Applying either fix alone causes regressions:
- Action ordering alone: 17 new failures (version ordering changes)  
- SHIFT-terminates-loop alone: 87 new failures (correct behavior on wrong ordering)
- Both together: same 17 new failures (advance loop doesn't match C's version management)

This requires a non-destructive Pop (like C's ts_stack_pop_count which returns slices without modifying the version head), plus renumber logic. This is a significant restructure.
